lastReleaseMessage: Restore dates.ts after a botched regex replacement in the...

barRaise.topFinding: Thirteenth daily run, seventh day with HEAD frozen at 890b493 (2026-06-13), and the working tree is byte-identical to the 06-19 pass: same four modified files, same two untracked .bak files, same two .bak files already tracked in HEAD, same uncommitted git-guard.ps1. Nothing in the project changed since the last report; today's canonical audit ran clean (zero doc-vs-code drift) and flagged only that *.bak is not gitignored, so the next release.ps1 git add -A will sweep more .bak residue in. The single HIGH is unchanged: one release.ps1 run ships the rebrand, the DESIGN cron-trigger correction, the v0.3.0 date fix, and git-guard.ps1, closes the HIGH, and reverts the verdict to healthy. The highest-leverage action this cycle is not in the project but in the cadence: pause bar-raise-scheduler-daily; it is now re-deriving an identical report against a frozen tree.

actions.title: Ship the whole working tree in one pass: `cd X:\YesAndScheduler; .\scripts\release.ps1` (or `git add -A; git commit`). Lands the rebrand, the DESIGN cron-trigger correction, the v0.3.0 date fix, and git-guard.ps1. origin/main...HEAD = 0 0, nothing external blocks it. Closes the reliability HIGH plus the maintainability/audit-loop MEDIUMs.

Pause bar-raise-scheduler-daily (or drop to twice-weekly) in the task registry until HEAD moves. The daily run is now re-deriving a byte-identical report; the ship recommendation has ridden ~9 reports.
Add *.bak to .gitignore and `git rm` the two tracked .bak files (scripts/discord-notify.ps1.bak, scripts/release.ps1.bak) so the next git add -A does not sweep more residue into HEAD.
